title: It Came Upon the Midnight Clear
author: Edmund Sears
story: Written by Unitarian pastor Edmund Sears during a period of social upheaval. Unique among carols for its focus on peace and the angels' song echoing through a weary world.

lines: It came upon the midnight clear, That glorious song of old, From angels bending near the earth, To touch their harps of gold
"Peace on the earth, good will to men, From Heaven's all gracious King
" The world in solemn stillness lay, To hear the angels sing
Still through the cloven skies they come With peaceful wings unfurled, And still their heavenly music floats O'er all the weary world
Above its sad and lowly plains, They bend on hov'ring wing, And ever over its Babel sounds The bless�d angels sing
Yet with the woes of sin and strife The world has suffered long
Beneath the angel strain have rolled Two thousand years of wrong
And man, at war with man, hears not The love\song which they bring
O hush the noise, ye men of strife And hear the angels sing
And ye, beneath life's crushing load, Whose forms are bending low, Who toil along the climbing way With painful steps and slow, Look now
for glad and golden hours Come swiftly on the wing
O rest beside the weary road, And hear the angels sing
the days are hast'ning on, By prophet\bards foretold, When with the ever circling years Comes round the age of gold
When peace shall over all the earth Its ancient splendors fling, And the whole world send back the song Which now the angels sing
